title: pimelea cinerea
intro:
Pimelea cinerea is a shrub native to Australia, belonging to the Thymelaeaceae family, commonly known as the "dwarf pimelea" due to its small stature and greyish foliage.

This plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits associated with Pimelea cinerea include its ability to soothe respiratory issues and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to support immune function. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves of the Pimelea cinerea plant can be used to make a tea, which is said to be effective in treating various ailments.
